task_completion.visual_evidence: In the email client's folder pane (usually on the left), there should be a folder explicitly named 'Dev-High-Priority'.
In the email client's folder pane, there should be a folder explicitly named 'User-Community'.

task_completion.description: Sort developer mailing list emails into 'Dev-High-Priority'
task_completion.visual_evidence: When viewing the contents of the 'Dev-High-Priority' folder, the email list should show messages containing '[SAdev]' or 'exmh-workers' in the sender or subject lines.

task_completion.description: Sort user/community mailing list emails into 'User-Community'
task_completion.visual_evidence: When viewing the contents of the 'User-Community' folder, the email list should show messages containing '[SAtalk]', '[ILUG]', or '[zzzzteana]' in the sender or subject lines.

task_completion.description: Flag emails in 'Dev-High-Priority' that mention release, patch, bug, or version
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'Dev-High-Priority' folder, exactly 3 emails should have a visual flag or star icon activated next to them.

task_completion.description: Send an email to council@apache.org reporting the number of flagged emails
task_completion.visual_evidence: A compose window, outbox, or 'Sent' folder showing an email addressed to 'council@apache.org' with the subject 'Release Triage Status' and a body that explicitly states the number '3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical email client to manage folders and emails
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an actual email client interface (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being used to create folders and move emails, rather than terminal windows using commands like 'mkdir' or 'mv'.

integrity.description: The agent composed the report email using the email client
integrity.visual_evidence: The report email should be visible in a standard email composition window or the client's Sent/Outbox folder, not just written to a text file or terminal.

integrity.description: Emails were actually moved into the folders, not just empty folders created
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Dev-High-Priority' and 'User-Community' folders should show a positive message count or display actual email items when selected, proving they are not empty.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create 'Dev-High-Priority' and 'User-Community' folders. Developer lists include 'sadev' and 'exmh-workers'. User lists include 'satalk', 'ilug', 'zzzzteana', and 'irr'. Exactly 3 developer emails contain the flagging keywords ('release', 'patch', 'bug', 'version'). Therefore, the agent must flag exactly 3 emails in the 'Dev-High-Priority' folder and report the number '3' in the email to 'council@apache.org'.
